meta.title: VPP Blockchain – Participant Points, Digital & Tamper-proof
meta.description: Collect participant points (VPP) tamper-proof on the blockchain. No paperwork – points are stored directly in your wallet. A project by HSBI.
meta.keywords: VPP, participant points, blockchain, HSBI, Bielefeld University of Applied Sciences, students, survey, wallet, smart contract

pageTitle.home: VPP Blockchain – Participant Points, Digital & Tamper-proof

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: The requested page does not exist.

common.error: An error occurred. Please try again or reload the page.

footer.title: Participant Points – Blockchain Project
footer.text: Participant points are issued as tamper-proof records on the blockchain.

hero.title: VPP points, digital — simple & tamper-proof
hero.subtitle: Take part in a study, confirm once at the end — and your point appears in your wallet for free. No paperwork, transparent at any time.

intro.title: What is this?
intro.text: VPP Blockchain is HSBI's system that records participant points (VPP) digitally. Instead of paper slips, you receive a tamper-proof point in your wallet after a study — without any personal data being stored. This lets you keep track at any time, lecturers can verify points quickly, and the process around registering your bachelor's thesis becomes easier. It's free for you; the system covers the fees in the background.

student.title: For students
student.text: See your points at any time — no more collecting paper receipts. Just confirm once at the end of the study and you're done.

lecturer.title: For lecturers
lecturer.text: Create studies, view points, manage surveys — all free of charge, with roles and explorer links.

docsCta.title: Questions? It's all in the docs.
docsCta.text: Guides for students and lecturers, technical background, FAQ and more — all in one place.

wallet.title: Wallet
wallet.noWallet: You don't have a wallet yet.
wallet.chooseMethod: How would you like to connect?

browser.title: Browser Wallet
browser.description: Your wallet is created and stored directly in your browser. No installation needed — one click is all it takes. Ideal if this is your first time using wallets.

metamask.title: MetaMask
metamask.description: MetaMask is a browser extension that manages your private key securely with encryption. Your wallet persists even after clearing browser data.
metamask.installHint: Install the MetaMask browser extension for free at
metamask.disconnectDescription: The connection to MetaMask will be removed. Your wallet remains in MetaMask — you can reconnect at any time.
metamask.reconnect: You can reconnect at any time.
metamask.networkChanged: MetaMask network was changed. The network does not affect signatures, but make sure everything works correctly.

create.title: Create Wallet
create.description: Generate a new wallet directly in your browser. No registration needed.
create.dialogTitle: Important: Before you create your wallet
create.dialogDescription: Please read the following points carefully. Your wallet and your points depend on it.
create.dialogInfo1Text: Your wallet is created and stored directly in your browser (in the so-called "Local Storage"). This means: It only exists in this browser on this device. There is no account, no password, and no email – only your private key.
create.dialogInfo2Text: The private key is the only way to access your wallet and your points. It works like a password that cannot be reset. Whoever has the key controls the wallet. If you lose it, your points are gone forever.
create.dialogInfo3Title: What do you need to do?
create.dialogInfo3Text: Save your private key immediately in a safe place: In a password manager (e.g., Bitwarden, 1Password) or written down on paper in a secure location. Never delete the browser cache or website data, as this will remove your wallet.
create.dialogInfo4Text: A browser wallet is the easiest option, but less secure than MetaMask. Your key is stored unencrypted in the browser. If you use a shared computer or want more security, use MetaMask instead.
create.dialogCheck1: I understand that the private key is my only access and cannot be recovered.
create.dialogCheck2: I will save the private key immediately in a secure place (password manager or paper).
create.dialogCheck3: I know that my wallet will be deleted if I clear the browser cache.

import.title: Import Wallet
import.description: Already have a private key? Import it to restore access on a new device.
import.error: Invalid private key format. The key must be 64 hex characters (with or without 0x prefix).

info.warning: Never share your private key with anyone. Store it securely!

reveal.title: Reveal Private Key
reveal.description: Your private key is like a password — anyone who has it can control your wallet and all your points.
reveal.check1: I am alone and nobody can see my screen.
reveal.check2: I will not show or share this key with anyone.
reveal.check3: I understand I must store this key securely.

delete.title: Delete Wallet
delete.description: The wallet will be permanently removed from your browser. Your points remain on the blockchain but will no longer be accessible without the private key.
delete.confirm: Did you back up your private key?
delete.confirmDescription: Without the private key you can no longer access your points. This action cannot be undone.

reset.title: Switch Wallet
reset.description: Replace your current wallet with an imported one. Your current wallet will be removed.
reset.warning: Make sure to back up your private key first!

claim.title: Claim Points
claim.noWallet: You need a wallet to claim points.

error.missingParams: The claim link is incomplete – survey ID, nonce, or token is missing. Please use the original link from the survey.
error.alreadyClaimed: You have already claimed points for this survey. Each wallet can only claim once per survey.
error.invalidToken: The claim link is invalid or has been tampered with. Please use the original link you received at the end of the survey.
error.invalidNonceFormat: The claim link is malformed (invalid nonce). Please open the survey again to receive a fresh link.
error.invalidTokenFormat: The claim link is malformed (invalid token). Please open the survey again to receive a fresh link.
error.nonceUsed: This claim link has already been used. Each link works exactly once. If you have not yet received your points, please contact vpstunden@hsbi.de.
error.surveyInactive: This survey has been deactivated and no longer accepts claims. Contact the survey administrator if you have questions.
error.maxClaims: The maximum number of participants for this survey has been reached. No more points can be awarded.
error.expired: Your signature has expired. Please reload the page and try again.
error.surveyNotFound: This survey is no longer registered. Please verify the claim link from the survey.
error.invalidSignature: The signature could not be verified. Please try again — make sure you are using the same wallet.
error.rateLimited: Too many requests in a short time. Please wait a moment and try again.
error.insufficientFunds: The system wallet does not have enough balance right now. Please try again in a few minutes or contact vpstunden@hsbi.de.
error.unauthorized: The system wallet currently lacks the required permission in the smart contract. Please contact vpstunden@hsbi.de.
error.network: Network error. The blockchain is currently unreachable. Please try again in a few seconds.
error.config: This survey is not fully configured. Please contact the study lead (vpstunden@hsbi.de).
error.generic: Points could not be claimed. Please check your internet connection and try again. If the problem persists, contact vpstunden@hsbi.de.

points.title: My Points
points.subtitle: View your current points balance and all past claims. Your points are read directly from the blockchain — tamper-proof and verifiable at any time.
points.dataError: Blockchain data could not be loaded. This may be due to an unstable internet connection or an overloaded RPC server. Please reload the page in a few seconds.

submittedBanner.title: Points Submitted
submittedBanner.description: Your wallet has been marked as "submitted". Your points have been presented and accepted for thesis admission.

points.noWallet: Create or import a wallet to view your points.
points.historyDescription: Each claim is stored as a transaction on the blockchain. Click the link to verify the transaction in the block explorer.
points.empty: No points yet. Complete a survey to earn your first points!

howItWorks.title: How does it work?
howItWorks.step1text: Participate in a study and confirm your participation at the end via the provided link.
howItWorks.step2text: The system awards your points automatically — free of charge, no crypto balance needed.
howItWorks.step3text: Your balance is read directly from the blockchain and visible here at any time.

explorer.title: Wallet Lookup
explorer.description: Look up points for any wallet address — e.g. to check your own balance on another device.
explorer.invalidAddress: Please enter a valid wallet address (starts with 0x followed by 40 characters).

auth.title: Admin Authentication
auth.description: Sign a message with your admin wallet to access the dashboard.

accessDenied.title: Access Denied
accessDenied.description: This area is restricted to lecturers with admin permissions on the smart contract. Your wallet does not currently have admin access.
accessDenied.hint: If you are a lecturer, please contact an existing admin (e.g. Prof. Haubrock) to have the role assigned to your wallet.

reactivate.error: Reactivation failed. Please try again later.

deactivateConfirm.title: Deactivate Survey
deactivateConfirm.description: Once deactivated, no more points can be claimed for this survey. This action is recorded on the blockchain.

templateDialog.title: Download Template
templateDialog.description: Choose the survey tool you need the template for. The template contains the claim button participants see at the end of the survey.
templateDialog.descriptionV2: Choose your survey tool. The template embeds the survey's HMAC key in a PHP snippet that mints a one-time claim link per participant on your SoSci/LimeSurvey server — the key never leaves that server.

sosci.title: SoSci Survey
sosci.description: Project file (.xml) for SoSci Survey. Import it as a new survey project.

limesurvey.title: LimeSurvey
limesurvey.description: Survey template (.lss) for LimeSurvey. Import via "Create survey → Import". The claim button is included in the end message.

keyDialog.title: Survey HMAC Key
keyDialog.description: This key signs the one-time claim links your SoSci/LimeSurvey server hands out. It is shown here only once — store it securely (e.g. in a password manager) and paste it into the downloaded template.
keyDialog.securityHint: This key is highly sensitive: anyone who knows it can mint valid claim links. Never send it via email or chat, and never paste it into an unprotected template.
keyDialog.rotateWarning: Warning: rotating immediately invalidates every claim link already in circulation. Only publish the new template once the new key is in place on SoSci/LimeSurvey.
keyDialog.rotateSuccess: New key generated. Update the template in your survey tool.
keyDialog.rotateError: Failed to rotate key. Please try again.
keyDialog.error: Could not load key. It may not have been created yet — re-register the survey or rotate.

register.title: Register New Survey
register.descriptionV2: After registration the system mints a fresh HMAC key for this survey. You will see it once right after — store it securely.
register.success: Survey registered successfully! The HMAC key will be shown next — store it securely.
register.error: Failed to register survey. Check if the ID is already taken and if the backend wallet has sufficient balance.
register.keyExplanation: On registration the server mints a fresh HMAC key. You will see it once right after — store it (e.g. in a password manager) and paste it into the SoSci/LimeSurvey template.

info.surveyId: Assigned automatically. You can change the value if needed.
info.title: A short name for the survey so you can easily identify it.
info.minterRole: The MINTER_ROLE allows the backend to award points. Only authorized wallets can use this function.

submissions.title: Student Submissions
submissions.description: Look up a student wallet and mark it as "submitted" once the points have been presented for thesis admission. This ensures the same wallet cannot be used twice.
submissions.invalidAddress: Please enter a valid wallet address (starts with 0x followed by 40 characters).
submissions.markSuccess: Wallet has been marked as submitted!
submissions.markError: Failed to mark wallet as submitted. It may already be marked or the backend wallet may have insufficient balance.
submissions.unmarkError: Failed to remove the submission mark. The wallet may no longer be marked.

confirmMark.title: Mark wallet as submitted
confirmMark.description: This will record on the blockchain that this wallet's points have been presented for thesis admission. The wallet cannot be used for admission again.

confirmUnmark.title: Remove submission mark
confirmUnmark.description: The submission mark will be removed from this wallet. It can then be presented for admission again.

submissions.infoTip: When students have collected enough points, they show their public key to the examiner. The examiner checks the balance and marks the wallet as "submitted" so it cannot be reused.

roles.title: Admin Role Management
roles.description: Manage which wallet addresses hold the admin role on the smart contract. Admins can register surveys, deactivate them, and add or remove other admins.
roles.noAdmins: No admins found. This may be due to a slow RPC connection – please reload the page.
roles.errorAdd: Failed to add admin. Check if the address is valid and the backend wallet has sufficient balance.
roles.errorRemove: Failed to remove admin. This address may no longer have admin permissions.
roles.infoTip: The ADMIN_ROLE is stored directly in the smart contract. Any admin can grant or revoke the admin role for other wallets. These changes are visible on the blockchain and immutably recorded.
roles.minterTooltip: This address belongs to the backend server. It is required to mint points and grant new admins, so it cannot be removed.

systemStatus.title: System Status
systemStatus.description: Overview of the backend wallet and blockchain connection status.
systemStatus.lowBalanceWarning: Low balance! Please top up the backend wallet to ensure continued operation.
systemStatus.loadError: Failed to load system status. Check if the backend is reachable.
systemStatus.infoTip: The backend wallet pays all transaction costs (gas fees) on the blockchain. When the balance runs out, no new claims or surveys can be processed. Top up ETH to the displayed address in time.

infoTips.wallet: A wallet is your digital keychain. It contains a public key (your address) and a private key (your password). Never share the private key.
infoTips.privateKey: Your private key is like a password — whoever has it controls your wallet. Store it securely and never share it.
infoTips.blockchain: The blockchain is a public, immutable ledger. Your points are stored there and can be verified by anyone.
infoTips.claim: A claim is the redemption of your survey participation. You sign a message with your wallet and the system awards you the points.
infoTips.baseFee: Base L2 is an Ethereum Layer 2 network with very low fees. A transaction costs less than $0.01.

legalForm.title: Legal Form
legalForm.text: Hochschule Bielefeld is a public corporation under German law.

representation.title: Statutory Representation
representation.text: It is legally represented by the President, Prof. Dr. Ingeborg Schramm-Wölk.

supervisory.title: Supervisory Authority
supervisory.text: Ministry of Culture and Science of the State of North Rhine-Westphalia (MKW), 40190 Düsseldorf

vat.title: VAT Identification Number
vat.text: pursuant to Section 27a of the German VAT Act: DE124007838

copyright.title: Copyright
copyright.text1: Unless otherwise stated, copyrights and usage rights for texts, graphics and images on all pages referring to this imprint are held by Hochschule Bielefeld.
copyright.text2: Creating, using, and distributing electronic copies requires permission.

own.title: Liability for Our Own Content
own.text: We are liable for the content of our own pages in accordance with statutory provisions. No guarantee or liability is assumed for the accuracy or completeness of the content provided on this website.

external.title: Liability for External Links
external.text1: Our offer contains links to external websites of third parties. We have no influence on the content of the linked pages; responsibility lies solely with the respective provider.
external.text2: We kindly request visitors to inform us immediately of any unlawful content on linked pages. If we become aware of legal violations, we will remove such links without delay.

intro.title: Summary
intro.text1: The protection of your personal data is important to us. This policy informs you about the data we process, the purposes, the legal basis, and your rights.
intro.text2: The use of our website is generally possible without providing personal data.

controller.title: Controller
controller.text: The controller within the meaning of the GDPR is:

serverLogs.title: Server Log Files
serverLogs.text: When accessing our website, the hosting provider automatically stores information in server log files.
items: IP address of the requesting device
serverLogs.legal: The legal basis is Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (legitimate interest in the secure provision of the website).

cookies.title: Cookies and Local Storage
cookies.text1: We only use technically necessary cookies or equivalent local storage techniques as required for the operation of the website (e.g., session ID, language settings).
cookies.text2: Legal basis for necessary cookies: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR.

web3.title: Web3 / Wallets and Blockchain
web3.text1: When creating or importing a wallet, we process your public wallet address and technical metadata to provide the service.
web3.text2: On-chain data is publicly accessible and permanently stored on the blockchain. Please do not store personal data in transaction metadata.
web3.text3: Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR (contractual purposes) and Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (IT security).

fonts.title: Fonts
fonts.text: Web fonts are served from our own servers (self-hosting). No connection to Google Fonts is established.

rights.title: Your Rights
rights.text: You have the following rights regarding your personal data:

items.name: Information (Art. 15 GDPR)
items.desc: You may request information about your processed data.

items.name: Rectification (Art. 16 GDPR)
items.desc: You may request the correction of inaccurate data.

items.name: Erasure (Art. 17 GDPR)
items.desc: You may request the deletion of your data.

items.name: Restriction (Art. 18 GDPR)
items.desc: You may request the restriction of processing.

items.name: Data Portability (Art. 20 GDPR)
items.desc: You may receive your data in a machine-readable format.

items.name: Withdrawal (Art. 7(3) GDPR)
items.desc: You may withdraw consent at any time.

rights.complaint: You also have the right to lodge a complaint with a data protection supervisory authority.

security.title: Security
security.text: We implement technical and organizational measures to protect your data against loss, destruction, access, alteration, or dissemination by unauthorized persons.

status.title: Conformance Status
status.text1: Hochschule Bielefeld strives to make its web offerings accessible in accordance with Section 3 BITV 2.0 and Directive (EU) 2016/2102.
status.text2: This web offering is currently partially conformant.

nonAccessible.title: Non-Accessible Content
items: Some images/icons do not yet have appropriate alternative text.
Dynamic content is not yet fully usable with screen readers.
nonAccessible.text: We are working on gradually addressing these issues.

feedback.title: Feedback and Contact
feedback.text: Have you noticed accessibility issues on our website? Please write to us — we will work on a prompt solution.

enforcement.title: Enforcement Procedure
enforcement.text: If you do not receive a satisfactory response within a reasonable period, you may contact the responsible enforcement or conciliation body.

creation.title: Creation of This Statement
creation.text: This statement was created on 14 October 2025 and last reviewed on 14 October 2025 (self-assessment).
